Tensor.name: shape
Tensor.description: Return the shape (size-per-dimension array) of the tensor.

Tensor.name: dtype
Tensor.description: Return the dtype string of the tensor.

Tensor.name: reshape
Tensor.description: Return a view with the given shape; total element count must match.

Tensor.name: permute
Tensor.description: Reorder all dimensions per the permutation array.

Tensor.name: expand
Tensor.description: Broadcast to a larger shape without copying memory.

Tensor.name: slice
Tensor.description: View a contiguous slice along the given dimension.

Tensor.name: unsqueeze
Tensor.description: Insert a size-1 dimension at the given position.

Tensor.name: squeeze
Tensor.description: Remove a size-1 dimension at the given position.

Tensor.name: select
Tensor.description: Select a single index along `dim`, removing that dimension.

Tensor.name: contiguous
Tensor.description: Return a row-major contiguous copy of the tensor.

Tensor.name: detach
Tensor.description: Return a copy detached from the autograd graph.

Tensor.name: backward
Tensor.description: Propagate gradients backward from this tensor.

Tensor.name: requires_grad
Tensor.description: Enable or disable gradient tracking on this tensor.

Tensor.name: grad
Tensor.description: Read the accumulated gradient of this leaf tensor.

Tensor.name: clone
Tensor.description: Return a deep copy of the tensor (separate storage).

Tensor.name: eq
Tensor.description: Element-wise equality comparison. Returns a boolean tensor.

Tensor.name: matmul
Tensor.description: Matrix multiplication; broadcasts on leading batch dimensions.

Tensor.name: sum
Tensor.description: Sum over `axis` (or the whole tensor); `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: mean
Tensor.description: Arithmetic mean over `axis` (or the whole tensor); `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: max
Tensor.description: Maximum over `axis` (or the whole tensor); `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: min
Tensor.description: Minimum over `axis` (or the whole tensor); `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: argmax
Tensor.description: Index of the maximum along `axis`; `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: argmin
Tensor.description: Index of the minimum along `axis`; `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: prod
Tensor.description: Product of elements over `axis` (or the whole tensor); `keep` retains reduced dims.

Tensor.name: softmax
Tensor.description: Softmax along `axis`, normalizing to a probability distribution.

Tensor.name: log_softmax
Tensor.description: Logarithm of softmax along `axis`, numerically stable.

Model.name: parameters
Model.description: Return the model's learnable parameter tensors.

Model.name: forward
Model.description: Run the model's forward block. Calling the model directly is equivalent.

Model.name: to
Model.description: Move the model's parameters to a device (`"cpu"`, `"gpu"`, `"webgpu"`) and return it.

Model.name: state_dict
Model.description: Return a serializable object of parameter tensors.

Model.name: load_state_dict
Model.description: Load parameter tensors from a previously saved object.

Model.name: save
Model.description: Save the model's weights to `path` (compact binary checkpoint). Reload into a same-architecture model with `load_model(model, path)`.

DataFrame.name: columns
DataFrame.description: Return the column names as an array of strings.

DataFrame.name: schema
DataFrame.description: Return the frame's schema (fields with names and data types).

DataFrame.name: explain
DataFrame.description: Return the logical query plan as a human-readable string.

DataFrame.name: select
DataFrame.description: Project a new frame from the given columns or `Column` expressions.

DataFrame.name: filter
DataFrame.description: Keep only rows matching a boolean `Column` (or SQL string) condition.

DataFrame.name: where
DataFrame.description: Alias for `filter`.

DataFrame.name: with_column
DataFrame.description: Return a new frame with an added or replaced column computed from `column`.

DataFrame.name: drop
DataFrame.description: Return a new frame without the named columns.

DataFrame.name: group_by
DataFrame.description: Group rows by the given columns, returning a `GroupedData` for aggregation.

DataFrame.name: order_by
DataFrame.description: Sort rows. Each spec is a column name/`Column`, or `{ col, desc }` for ordering.

DataFrame.name: sort
DataFrame.description: Alias for `order_by`.

DataFrame.name: limit
DataFrame.description: Return at most `count` rows, skipping the first `offset` rows.

DataFrame.name: head
DataFrame.description: Return the first `n` rows as a new frame (pandas-style preview).

DataFrame.name: distinct
DataFrame.description: Return a frame with duplicate rows removed.

DataFrame.name: union
DataFrame.description: Concatenate the rows of another frame with matching column types.

DataFrame.name: union_all
DataFrame.description: Concatenate rows of another frame, keeping duplicates.

DataFrame.name: join
DataFrame.description:
Join with another frame on one or more key columns. `how` is one of
`INNER`, `LEFT`, `RIGHT`, or `FULL`.

DataFrame.name: collect
DataFrame.description: Execute the plan and return all rows as an array of objects.

DataFrame.name: to_array
DataFrame.description: Alias for `collect`.

DataFrame.name: count
DataFrame.description: Execute the plan and return the row count.

DataFrame.name: show
DataFrame.description: Execute and print the first `n` rows as a formatted table; returns the text.

DataFrame.name: chunks
DataFrame.description: Execute and stream results as an async iterator of data chunks.

DataFrame.name: to_tensor
DataFrame.description:
Materialize the (optionally selected) numeric columns into a 2-D tensor of
shape `[rows, columns]`. Non-numeric columns raise — encode them first.

DataFrame.name: to_array
DataFrame.description: Alias for `collect` — execute and return rows as an array of objects.

DataFrame.name: encode
DataFrame.description:
Encode a categorical column to integer ids, returning `[encoded_tensor,
classes_array]`. Pass `classes=` to reuse ids fitted on another frame.

GroupedData.name: agg
GroupedData.description:
Apply aggregate `Column` expressions (e.g. `sum`, `avg`, `count`) over each
group, returning a `DataFrame` of group keys and aggregates.

Column.name: alias
Column.description: Rename the column's output to `name`.

Column.name: as
Column.description: Alias for `alias`.

Column.name: add
Column.description: Arithmetic addition with another column or value.

Column.name: sub
Column.description: Arithmetic subtraction with another column or value.

Column.name: mul
Column.description: Arithmetic multiplication with another column or value.

Column.name: div
Column.description: Arithmetic division with another column or value.

Column.name: eq
Column.description: Equality comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: ne
Column.description: Inequality comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: lt
Column.description: Less-than comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: le
Column.description: Less-than-or-equal comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: gt
Column.description: Greater-than comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: ge
Column.description: Greater-than-or-equal comparison, producing a boolean column.

Column.name: and
Column.description: Logical AND of two boolean columns.

Column.name: or
Column.description: Logical OR of two boolean columns.

Column.name: not
Column.description: Logical negation of a boolean column.

Column.name: is_null
Column.description: True where the column value is null.

Column.name: is_not_null
Column.description: True where the column value is not null.

Column.name: like
Column.description: SQL `LIKE` match against a string pattern.

Column.name: between
Column.description: True where the value lies in the inclusive range `[low, high]`.

Column.name: isin
Column.description: True where the value is one of the given values.

Column.name: cast
Column.description: Cast the column to another data type.

Array.name: push
Array.description: Add `x` to the end of the array and return the new length.

Array.name: pop
Array.description: Remove and return the last element.

Array.name: shift
Array.description: Remove and return the first element.

Array.name: unshift
Array.description: Insert `x` at the front and return the new length.

Array.name: splice
Array.description:
Remove `count` elements starting at `start`, inserting `items` in their place, and
return the removed elements.

Array.name: slice
Array.description: Return a shallow copy of the range `[start, end)`. Negative indices count from the end.

Array.name: concat
Array.description: Return a new array with `arrays` appended to this one.

Array.name: index_of
Array.description: Return the index of the first occurrence of `x`, or `-1` if absent.

Array.name: last_index_of
Array.description: Return the index of the last occurrence of `x`, or `-1` if absent.

Array.name: includes
Array.description: True when `x` is present.

Array.name: reverse
Array.description: Reverse the array in place and return it.

Array.name: sort
Array.description: Sort the array in place and return it.

Array.name: join
Array.description: Join the elements into a string separated by `sep` (default `","`).

Array.name: map
Array.description: Return a new array with `fn` applied to every element.

Array.name: filter
Array.description: Return a new array of the elements for which `fn` returns true.

Array.name: reduce
Array.description: Fold the array left-to-right with `fn`.

Array.name: for_each
Array.description: Call `fn` for every element.

Array.name: find
Array.description: Return the first element for which `fn` returns true, or `null`.

Array.name: find_index
Array.description: Return the index of the first element for which `fn` returns true, or `-1`.

Array.name: flat_map
Array.description: Map every element with `fn` and flatten the result one level.

Array.name: length
Array.description: Number of elements.

String.name: to_upper_case
String.description: Return the string with every character upper-cased.

String.name: to_lower_case
String.description: Return the string with every character lower-cased.

String.name: trim
String.description: Return the string with leading and trailing whitespace removed.

String.name: trim_start
String.description: Return the string with leading whitespace removed.

String.name: trim_end
String.description: Return the string with trailing whitespace removed.

String.name: replace
String.description: Return a copy with the first occurrence of `old` replaced by `new`.

String.name: replace_all
String.description: Return a copy with every occurrence of `old` replaced by `new`.

String.name: starts_with
String.description: True when the string begins with `prefix`.

String.name: ends_with
String.description: True when the string ends with `suffix`.

String.name: index_of
String.description: Return the index of the first occurrence of `sub`, or `-1` if absent.

String.name: includes
String.description: True when `sub` occurs anywhere in the string.

String.name: slice
String.description: Return the substring `[start, end)`. Negative indices count from the end.

String.name: pad_start
String.description: Pad the front with `fill` until the string reaches `len`.

String.name: pad_end
String.description: Pad the end with `fill` until the string reaches `len`.

Map.name: get
Map.description: Return the value stored under `key`, or `undefined`.

Map.name: set
Map.description: Store `value` under `key` and return the map.

Map.name: delete
Map.description: Remove `key`, returning whether it was present.

builtins.name: tensor
builtins.description: Construct a tensor from a literal value, array, or nested array. Accepts `dtype`, `device`, `grad` options.

builtins.name: zeros
builtins.description: Create a tensor of the given shape filled with `0`.

builtins.name: ones
builtins.description: Create a tensor of the given shape filled with `1`.

builtins.name: empty
builtins.description: Allocate a tensor of the given shape without initializing its contents.

builtins.name: full
builtins.description: Create a tensor of the given shape filled with the provided scalar `value`.

signature.display: full(shape: Array, value: float, ...options: Object) -> Tensor

builtins.name: randn
builtins.description: Sample a tensor of the given shape from the standard normal distribution.

builtins.name: arange
builtins.description: Half-open integer range tensor `[start, end)` with optional `step`.

signature.display: arange(start: int, end?: int, step?: int, ...options: Object) -> Tensor

builtins.name: eye
builtins.description: Identity matrix of size `n × m` (or `n × n` if `m` omitted).

signature.display: eye(n: int, m?: int, ...options: Object) -> Tensor

builtins.name: linspace
builtins.description: Evenly spaced values between `start` and `end`, inclusive, with `steps` points.

signature.display: linspace(start: float, end: float, steps: int, ...options: Object) -> Tensor

builtins.name: randperm
builtins.description: Random permutation of integers `0..n-1`.

builtins.name: zeros_like
builtins.description: Zero tensor with the same shape, dtype, and device as the input.

builtins.name: ones_like
builtins.description: Tensor of ones with the same shape, dtype, and device as the input.

builtins.name: empty_like
builtins.description: Uninitialized tensor with the same shape, dtype, and device as the input.

builtins.name: full_like
builtins.description: Constant-filled tensor matching the shape, dtype, and device of the input.

builtins.name: randn_like
builtins.description: Standard-normal sample with the same shape, dtype, and device as the input.

builtins.name: where
builtins.description: Element-wise conditional selection: pick from `a` where `condition` is true, else from `b`.

signature.display: where(condition: Tensor, a: Tensor, b: Tensor) -> Tensor

builtins.name: cat
builtins.description: Concatenate tensors along an existing dimension.

builtins.name: stack
builtins.description: Stack tensors along a new dimension.

builtins.name: sum
builtins.description: Aggregate `Column` computing the sum of a column within a `group_by(...).agg(...)`.

builtins.name: max
builtins.description: Aggregate `Column` computing the maximum of a column within a `group_by(...).agg(...)`.

builtins.name: min
builtins.description: Aggregate `Column` computing the minimum of a column within a `group_by(...).agg(...)`.

builtins.name: range
builtins.description: Integer range: returns an array `[start..stop)` with optional `step`.

signature.display: range(start?: int, stop?: int, step: int = 1) -> int[]

builtins.name: print
builtins.description: Print one or more values to the runtime output, separated by a space.

builtins.name: compile
builtins.description: Compile a model or function to a backend (`cpu`/`gpu`/`wasm`/`webgpu`). `input` provides an example for shape inference and tuning.

builtins.description: Compose modules into a feed-forward pipeline. The output of each module is fed to the next.

builtins.name: DataFrame
builtins.description:
Build a lazy `DataFrame` from named column arrays, one named argument per
column: `DataFrame(name=["a", "b"], age=[30, 40])`. Column types are inferred
from the values. The frame records a query plan and is only executed when
materialized with `collect`, `to_array`, `count`, `show`, or `chunks`.

builtins.name: col
builtins.description:
Reference a column by name in a `DataFrame` expression, returning a `Column`
that can be transformed and compared. Use a dotted name (`"t.id"`) to qualify a
table alias.

builtins.name: lit
builtins.description:
Wrap a constant value as a `Column` literal so it can be combined with other
columns in expressions.

builtins.name: expr
builtins.description:
Parse a scalar SQL string into a `Column`, e.g. `expr("price * 1.1")`. Bound
against the frame's schema at build time.

builtins.name: avg
builtins.description: Aggregate `Column` computing the mean of a column within a `group_by(...).agg(...)`.

builtins.name: count
builtins.description: Aggregate `Column` counting non-null values of a column within `agg(...)`.

builtins.name: count_star
builtins.description: Aggregate `Column` counting all rows (`COUNT(*)`) within `agg(...)`.

builtins.name: register_columns_table
builtins.description:
Register named column arrays as a SQL-addressable table and return its generated
table name, one named argument per column: `register_columns_table(name=["a"], age=[30])`.
Use the returned name inside `expr("... FROM <name>")`.

builtins.name: backtest
builtins.description: Run a vectorized cross-sectional backtest over a price `DataFrame` shaped time × asset (numeric columns are the assets; a date/index column is dropped automatically). `signal` selects a trading signal (`"momentum"`, `"mean_reversion"`, `"zscore"`) and `portfolio` a position rule (`"equal_weight"`, `"cross_sectional"`, `"long_short"`); either may instead be a handle from `momentum(...)`, `long_short(...)`, etc. Returns a record with `.metrics` (a map of `sharpe`, `sortino`, `maxDrawdown`, `calmar`, `hitRate`, `turnover`), `.equity` and `.port_returns` (DataFrames), and `.weights`.

builtins.name: walk_forward
builtins.description: Walk-forward (out-of-sample) backtest: split the series into `folds` segments after an initial `min_train_fraction` training window and stitch the per-fold out-of-sample returns. Same arguments and result shape as `backtest`.

builtins.name: momentum
builtins.description: Build a momentum signal handle (trailing return over `lookback` periods) to pass to `backtest`/`walk_forward` as `signal=`.

builtins.name: mean_reversion
builtins.description: Build a mean-reversion signal handle (the negated `lookback` momentum) for use as `signal=`.

builtins.name: zscore
builtins.description: Build a z-score signal handle (rolling standardized price over `window`) for use as `signal=`.

builtins.name: equal_weight
builtins.description: Portfolio handle weighting every active asset equally by sign, for use as `portfolio=`.

builtins.name: cross_sectional
builtins.description: Portfolio handle that demeans the signal across assets and scales to unit gross exposure, for use as `portfolio=`.

builtins.name: long_short
builtins.description: Portfolio handle going long the top `fraction` and short the bottom `fraction` of ranked assets, for use as `portfolio=`.

builtins.name: sharpe
builtins.description: Annualized Sharpe ratio of a returns array or a single-column returns `DataFrame`.

builtins.name: deflated_sharpe
builtins.description: Deflated Sharpe ratio — the probability the strategy's Sharpe is real after accounting for the count and dispersion of `trial_sharpes` searched over (guards against selection bias).

builtins.name: pbo
builtins.description: Probability of Backtest Overfitting via combinatorially symmetric cross-validation over a time × trial matrix (rows of returns, one column per candidate strategy). Accepts a matrix or a `DataFrame`.

builtins.name: min_track_record_length
builtins.description: Minimum count of observations needed before the observed Sharpe exceeds `target_sharpe` at the given `confidence`.

builtins.name: risk_parity
builtins.description: Equal-risk-contribution portfolio weights for a covariance matrix. Passing a returns `DataFrame` estimates the sample covariance first. Returns a weight array.

builtins.name: hrp
builtins.description: Hierarchical Risk Parity weights — cluster assets by correlation and allocate by recursive bisection. Accepts a covariance matrix or a returns `DataFrame`.

builtins.name: mean_variance
builtins.description: Mean-variance optimal weights for expected returns `mu` and covariance `cov` (a matrix or a returns `DataFrame`), normalized to unit gross exposure.

builtins.name: quill
builtins.description: Parse and type-check a Quill product definition from a source string and return a product handle. Call `.price(rate=..., spot=..., vol=..., paths?=..., seed?=..., greeks?=...)` on it to run the Monte-Carlo pricer; the result has `.price`, `.standard_error`, and a `.greeks` map (`delta`, `vega`, `rho`, …). `greeks` is `"price-only"`, `"first-order"`, or `"full"`.

builtins.name: load_quill
builtins.description: Like `quill`, but read the Quill product definition from a file `path`. Returns the same product handle with a `.price(...)` method and a `.name` field.

builtins.name: adf_test
builtins.description: Augmented Dickey-Fuller unit-root test. Returns a record with `statistic`, `criticalValues`, and `stationary` (true when the statistic is below the critical value).

builtins.name: kpss_test
builtins.description: KPSS stationarity test (null hypothesis: the series is stationary). Returns `statistic`, `criticalValues`, `stationary`. Complements `adf_test`.

builtins.name: hurst_exponent
builtins.description: Hurst exponent from rescaled-range analysis. `< 0.5` mean-reverting, `~0.5` random walk, `> 0.5` trending.

builtins.name: half_life
builtins.description: Ornstein-Uhlenbeck mean-reversion half-life (in periods) estimated by regressing the change on the lagged level.

builtins.name: engle_granger
builtins.description: Engle-Granger two-step cointegration test: regress `dependent` on `regressors`, then ADF-test the residual. Returns `statistic`, `criticalValues`, `cointegrated`, `hedgeRatio`, and `spread`.

builtins.name: johansen
builtins.description: Johansen cointegration test on a matrix of price levels. Returns `eigenvalues`, `traceStatistics`, `maxEigenStatistics`, and the estimated cointegration `rank`.

builtins.name: cusum_events
builtins.description: Symmetric CUSUM filter — returns the indices where the cumulative deviation exceeds `threshold`, used to sample structural-shift events.

builtins.name: sadf
builtins.description: Supremum ADF statistic — the max ADF over expanding windows, a test for explosive (bubble) behavior.

builtins.name: bsadf
builtins.description: Backward-SADF series — the running SADF at each point, for dating the start/end of explosive regimes.

builtins.name: kalman_filter
builtins.description: Linear Kalman filter over a state-space `spec` (transition, observation, process/measurement noise). Returns filtered `states`, `covariances`, and one-step innovations.

builtins.name: kalman_smoother
builtins.description: Rauch-Tung-Striebel smoother — the full-sample smoothed state matrix for the same state-space `spec` as `kalman_filter`.

builtins.name: dynamic_beta
builtins.description: Time-varying hedge ratio / beta via a Kalman filter (random-walk coefficients). Returns the per-period `states` (betas) — the workhorse for dynamic pairs trading.

builtins.name: fit_garch
builtins.description: Fit a GARCH(1,1) volatility model by maximum likelihood. Returns a record with `params` (`omega`, `alpha`, `beta`), `log_likelihood`, and fitted `variances`.

builtins.name: garch_forecast
builtins.description: Forecast conditional variance `horizon` steps ahead from GARCH `params` (as returned by `fit_garch`).

builtins.name: garch_volatility
builtins.description: In-sample conditional volatility path (standard deviation per period) for the given GARCH `params`.

builtins.name: tick_bars
builtins.description: Aggregate a `ticks` DataFrame (`price`, `volume`) into OHLC bars of fixed tick count. Returns a bar `DataFrame`.

builtins.name: volume_bars
builtins.description: Information-driven bars sampled every fixed traded `volume_per_bar`. Returns a bar `DataFrame`.

builtins.name: dollar_bars
builtins.description: Bars sampled every fixed traded dollar value — the most sample-stationary bar type. Returns a bar `DataFrame`.

builtins.name: tick_rule
builtins.description: Lee-Ready tick rule — signs each trade `+1`/`-1` by price change to infer aggressor side.

builtins.name: roll_spread
builtins.description: Roll's implied effective bid-ask spread from the serial covariance of price changes.

builtins.name: amihud
builtins.description: Amihud illiquidity — average of `|return| / dollar_volume`, a price-impact-per-dollar measure.

builtins.name: kyle_lambda
builtins.description: Kyle's lambda — price impact per signed volume, estimated by regressing price changes on signed order flow.

builtins.name: vpin
builtins.description: Volume-synchronized Probability of Informed Trading — order-flow-toxicity series over volume buckets.

methods.name: line
methods.description: Draw a line chart for ordered values or trends. Use y=[...] for multiple series and color= to group DataFrame rows. Add a dashed reference line with hline=3.5 (horizontal) or vline=100 (vertical) — pass an int, float, or array, and label/color them with hline_label="μ = 3.5", hline_color="#e06c75". Use dash=true to dash every series. Pass animate=true to reveal the line left→right with Play/Pause, a scrubber, loop, and speed controls (honours reduced-motion). Pass frame="step" to morph the curve between keyframes (one per distinct frame value), tweening vertices over time with a frame scrubber. Pace the motion with easing="linear"|"ease"|"ease-in-out"|"cubic", repeat with loop=true, run faster/slower with speed=0.5|1|2|4, and auto-start with autoplay=true (otherwise the chart rests on its final frame as a static poster until you press Play, so exports and screenshots stay complete).

methods.name: scatter
methods.description: Plot numeric X/Y observations to inspect relationships, clusters, and outliers. Use color= to split DataFrame groups. Pass animate=true to reveal points left→right with transport controls. Pass frame="year" with key="country" to morph the marks between keyframes (Gapminder-style): each distinct frame value becomes a keyframe, marks matched by key smoothly interpolate their x/y (and size/color), and marks that enter or leave fade in/out. The transport label shows the current frame value and the scrubber seeks by frame; reduced-motion snaps between frames without tweening. Tune the motion with easing="linear"|"ease"|"ease-in-out"|"cubic", loop=true/false, and speed=0.5|1|2|4; until you press Play (or set autoplay=true) the chart holds its last frame as a static poster so exports stay complete.

methods.description: Plot X/Y observations with marker area scaled by a third numeric variable. Useful for spend, revenue, or segment size. Pass frame="year" with key="country" to morph the bubbles between keyframes: marks matched by key interpolate their x/y/size/color over time, entering/leaving marks fade, and the transport scrubber seeks by frame value (reduced-motion snaps without tweening). Pace it with easing="linear"|"ease"|"ease-in-out"|"cubic", loop=true/false, and speed=0.5|1|2|4; the chart holds its last frame as a static poster until you press Play (or set autoplay=true).
